title: Chicken Road
tagline: Cross lane by lane, multiplier rising — cash out before you get hit.
mechanic: Each lane is survived with probability p (set by difficulty). After n lanes the multiplier is RTP ÷ p^n. The only choice is when to stop. Lower difficulty = more lanes, smaller steps; daredevil = rarely survive, huge steps.
showcases: difficulty-driven hazard, identical EV math to Crash
mathTest: Constant-EV: every cash-out lane has expected value RTP, so `--compare` across stop-points is flat at 0.99 for each difficulty.
